title: A New Pill That Fights Bad Cholesterol
summary: Scientists have tested a new pill called enlicitide that can lower bad cholesterol by 60%. Bad cholesterol, called LDL, can build up in arteries and cause heart attacks or strokes. The pill was given to people in a big study, and it helped reduce their cholesterol levels. This is important because many people with heart disease have high cholesterol, and current medicines like statins or injections are not always enough. The new pill is taken once a day, making it easier for patients. If approved, it could help millions of people stay healthier by preventing heart problems. The study was led by a doctor and sponsored by a drug company, showing it might be a safe and effective option in the future.

questions.question: Who led the study on the new pill?

background_read: Cholesterol is a waxy substance in your blood. Bad cholesterol, or LDL, can stick to artery walls and block blood flow, leading to heart attacks or strokes. Doctors use medicines like statins to lower it, but sometimes these aren't enough. A new pill called enlicitide might help by reducing LDL more effectively and being easier to take. This could make it simpler for people to stay healthy and avoid heart problems.
Article_Structure: WHO: Dr. Navar and other scientists from UT Southwestern Medical Center and Merck & Co. Inc.
WHAT: A new pill called enlicitide that lowers bad cholesterol by 60% in a study.
WHERE: In the United States, tested in a clinical trial.
WHY: To help people reduce their risk of heart attacks and strokes by making cholesterol control easier.

perspectives.description: Doctors see this pill as a way to better prevent heart disease by lowering cholesterol more effectively.
